reason: The statement applies because the text describes the actions and consequences of Jeroboam, who rebelled against Solomon and became king over Israel. It details how Jeroboam sinned by setting up golden calves for worship and appointing priests from the lowest of the people, leading the people into idolatry. As a result, the text depicts the Lord's judgment upon Jeroboam and his house, including the death of his son due to his disobedience.
